name: Galatine
description: Galatine, also known as Calatine, is the sword of Sir Gawaine in Arthurian legend. While often overshadowed by Excalibur, Galatine is a potent symbol of Gawaine's own prowess and chivalry. Its origins are somewhat obscure compared to Arthur's sword, but it is generally accepted that Galatine was a blade of exceptional quality, granting its wielder significant advantage in battle. It is typically described as being a shining, well-crafted sword, perfectly balanced and extremely sharp. Some accounts suggest that Galatine possessed a magical property, its power waxing and waning with the sun, making Gawaine nearly invincible at noon. The sword's significance lies in its representation of Gawaine's honorable nature and his role as one of the most important knights of the Round Table. Losing Galatine or wielding it improperly would have been a grave dishonor for Gawaine, reflecting poorly on his character and knighthood. The sword served as an extension of his virtue and a testament to his skill as a warrior. It played a crucial role in many of Gawaine's adventures and battles, solidifying his reputation as a formidable and respected knight. The sword's legacy is intertwined with Gawaine's, serving as a reminder of his bravery, loyalty, and commitment to the ideals of chivalry.
subtitle: Gawaine's sun-powered blade of honor
origin: Forged by Wayland the Smith, given to Gawaine.
currentLocation: Unknown, likely lost after Gawaine's death.

keyMyths.title: The Noon's Blessing
keyMyths.description: Galatine's power reached its peak at midday, granting Gawaine superhuman strength and resilience. This made him nearly unbeatable during the hours of high noon.
keyMyths.source: Sir Gawaine and the Green Knight

keyMyths.title: Gawaine's Oath
keyMyths.description: Gawaine swore to wield Galatine only in the service of good and justice. Breaking this oath would diminish the sword's power and bring shame upon him.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends.

sources.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
sources.work: Sir Gawaine and the Green Knight
sources.description: A Middle English alliterative romance depicting Gawaine's adventures.
